content: By the time I graduated high school, my trouble with my parents had grown so that my dad advised one of two things, either to join the Army or to get a job and go out on my own. I joined the Army in 1981 in the capacity of what was called as job nomenclature, Pershing Electronics Materials Specialist, working with a nuclear weapon which was deployed in Germany at the time by the U.S. Army. In this capacity, I went to Germany with the Army for the duration of my two-year enlistment, after which time I basically remained in Germany and did not return to the States completelyUntil 1982, thus spending an entire 10 years living abroad in Germany.
